description: 8-turn conversation where the user provides information, then corrects it multiple times. Tests whether the memory manager properly updates rather than accumulating contradictory entries.

turns.user_message: I need help setting up my deployment. We're deploying to AWS using Docker containers on ECS.
Our database is MySQL 8.0 running on RDS.
Wait, I need to correct myself — we actually switched to PostgreSQL last month. Sorry about that.
turns.response_context: User correcting database type. MUST update, not store a new memory alongside MySQL.

turns.user_message: The app is a Python Flask application with about 20 API endpoints.
Actually, we migrated to FastAPI last sprint. I keep mixing them up.
turns.response_context: Another correction. MUST update the Flask memory to FastAPI.

turns.user_message: So to summarize — what do you have recorded about our setup?

expected_ops.must_recall_correctly: PostgreSQL (not MySQL), FastAPI (not Flask), AWS ECS with Docker

turns.response_context: Tests that corrections were properly applied. Must have PostgreSQL and FastAPI, NOT MySQL and Flask.

turns.user_message: Perfect, that's right. One more thing — we're also using Redis for caching and Celery for background tasks.
Thanks for keeping track of all that!
turns.response_context: Casual acknowledgment. Should NOT store anything.

correct_epistemic_behavior: Store initial factual information about the deployment
When user corrects MySQL to PostgreSQL, UPDATE the existing memory rather than creating a new one
When user corrects Flask to FastAPI, UPDATE the existing memory rather than creating a new one
At turn 6, correctly recall PostgreSQL and FastAPI (not MySQL/Flask)
Do NOT store the casual 'thanks' message at turn 8
Final memory should be clean — no contradictory entries about MySQL or Flask
incorrect_behaviors: Creating new memories for corrections instead of updating existing ones
Having both MySQL and PostgreSQL in final memory
Having both Flask and FastAPI in final memory
Storing the casual acknowledgment at turn 8
Not recognizing corrections as updates to existing information

key_claims.claim: The system properly updates on user corrections
The system stores casual chat messages
